---
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---
# Appendix

## Methods 

### Volcano plot
A Volcano plot is a valuable method for comparing gene expression differences between two groups. Initially, the fold change (FC) value is computed using the formula:
$$
\text{FC} = \frac{\text{mean}(\text{group 1})}{\text{mean}(\text{group 2})}
$$ 
Next, a Shapiro-Wilk test assesses the normality of the data distribution, guiding the selecting of appropriate statistical tests to determine significance [@ShapiroWilk]. Given the non-normal distribution indicated by the Shapiro-Wilk test, the Wilcoxon rank-sum test is employed for further analysis. To account for multiple comparisons, the Bonferroni correction is applied to the p-values. 
This approach allows the visualization of gene expression differences between LUAD and LUSC patients, with LUAD as the first group and LUSC as the second. Gene expression data from the pancancer data set was utilized. 

### Detailed Explanation of Binary Logistic Regression
As already explained, the logistic regression model is represented as: 
$$
\log\left(\frac{p_1}{1 - p_1}\right) = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X_1 + ... + \beta_i X_i
$$ 
where:
 \( p \) is the probability of the event,
 \( \beta_0 \) is the intercept,
 \( \beta_1, \beta_i \) are the coefficients for the predictors \( X_1, X_i \).
These results are tranformed by the logit function \( \log(\text{odds}) = \log\left(\frac{p_1}{1 - p_1}\right) \) ranging from \(-\infty\) to \(+\infty\). A regression line is fitted to these data points, representing the relationship between the logistic probability and the predictors. The initial data points are projected onto this line to determine coefficients and a new logit value is calculated. The sigmoidal curve is plotted by applying the logistic function to the new logit value, thereby obtaining a value between 1 and 0: 
$$
p = \frac{e^{\text{log(odds)}}}{1 + e^{\text{log(odds)}}}. 
$$
The best-fitting regression line is identified using the "maximum likelihood" method, which calculates probabilities for each data point. To determine the optimal line, likelihoods are computed for all possible lines with slight adjustments to their parameters in each iteration. The line achieving the highest likelihood value is considered the best fit [@logisticregression]. 

## Plots
\begin{figure}[htbp]
    \centering
    \includegraphics{"C:/Users/kolbc/Desktop/figures_bioinfo/303.png"}
    \caption[\textbf{UMAP GSEA LUAD-patients}.]{\textbf{UMAP GSEA patients}. This figure shows the clustering of LUAD-patients using the GSEA data on foldchange values between healthy and cancerous tissues using both hallmark and oncogenic signature genesets, via UMAP.}
    \label{303}
\end{figure}
\begin{figure}[htbp]
    \centering
    \includegraphics{"C:/Users/kolbc/Desktop/figures_bioinfo/204.png"}
    \caption[\textbf{LUAD patients compared to LUSC patients}.]{\textbf{enrichGo analysis of 1,500 highest and 1,500 lowest FC values of comparisson of LUAD and LUSC}. (A) This shows processes associated with the 1,500 gene with lowest FC value. (B) Here, upregulated processes associated with the 1,500 gene with highest FC value can be seen.}
    \label{204}
\end{figure}
\newpage
## Binary logistic regression

\textbf{Table logistic regression model variables and stats}
\begin{table}[h]
\begin{tabular}{l|l|l|l}
                                        & \textbf{Estimate} & \textbf{Standard error} & \textbf{p-value}  \\
Intercept                               & -0.1503  & 0.1785         & 0.399783 \\
HALLMARK\_MITOTIC\_SPINDLE              & 4.2191   & 1.1547         & 0.000258 \\
HALLMARK\_MTORC1\_SIGNALING             & -4.4199  & 0.9632         & 4.46e-06 \\
HALLMARK\_NOTCH\_SIGNALING              & 1.9501   & 0.7373         & 0.008176 \\
HALLMARK\_OXIDATIVE\_PHOSPHORYLATION    & 2.1717   & 0.4644         & 2.92e-06 \\
HALLMARK\_PROTEIN\_SECRETION            & 2.6732   & 0.7092         & 0.000164 \\
HALLMARK\_WNT\_BETA\_CATENIN\_SIGNALING & -4.5210  & 0.7047         & 1.40e-10 \\
E2F1\_UP.V1\_U                          & -5.1325  & 1.4770         & 0.000511 \\
EIF4E\_DN                               & 3.8867   & 1.0141         & 0.000127 \\
RPS14\_DN.V1\_DN                        & -5.5243  & 1.5983         & 0.000547 \\
STK33\_NOMO\_UP                         & -4.0512  & 1.0705         & 0.000154 \\
TBK1.DF\_DN                             & -5.5171  & 1.0151         & 5.47e-08 \\
TBK1.DN.48HRS\_DN                       & 2.8749   & 0.7334         & 8.85e-05 \\
VEGF\_A\_UP.V1\_DN                      & 4.1303   & 1.4051         & 0.003288
\end{tabular}
\label{Tabelle1}
\end{table}
